headings.text: Can I designate my gift to a specific project?
How do I sign up to volunteer?
Who is eligible to apply for grants?
Can my school or class visit the field office?
How do I report a wetland concern or fish kill?

paragraphs.text: Whether you're proposing a partnership, applying for a grant, joining a volunteer crew, or asking a question about Gulf Coast wetlands, our team is glad to hear from you. Reach us by mail, by email, or through the form below.

Our office sits along the western shore of the Laguna Madre, fifteen minutes south of downtown Corpus Christi. Visitors are welcome during business hours; please call ahead if you plan to tour the native plant nursery or restoration lab.
All mail is reviewed within five business days.
Six full-time staff coordinate restoration, education, grants, and field operations across the Texas and Louisiana Gulf Coast.
Yes. Brackwater Marsh Conservancy is a registered 501(c)(3). All donations are tax-deductible to the fullest extent permitted by U.S. law. A receipt is emailed within 48 hours.
Absolutely. During checkout, choose from restoration, education, or the Coastal Resilience Fellowship. Restricted gifts of $1,000+ may be tracked with quarterly impact updates.
Visit our Volunteer page, review the eight open program roles, and submit an interest form. A coordinator follows up within five business days to schedule onboarding and orientation.
Most roles require no prior experience — just curiosity and reliability. Specialized roles like Bird Survey Technician and Water Quality Sampler include free training sessions each spring.
The Seed Fund accepts rolling applications. The Education Initiative opens each January, and the Fellowship cycle opens each March. See the Grants page for specific deadlines and required materials.
Eligibility varies by program. Most grants fund Gulf Coast nonprofits, educators, tribes, and emerging scientists working within Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, or the Florida Panhandle.
Yes — we host K-12 group tours on Tuesdays and Thursdays during the school year. Email Aisha Bellamy at least three weeks in advance to coordinate scheduling and curriculum alignment.
For active emergencies, contact Texas Parks & Wildlife first. For ongoing observations or restoration questions, email field@brackwatermarsh.org with location, date, and any photos.
Use the form below for general inquiries. For grant submissions or volunteer applications, please use the dedicated forms on those program pages.
Restoring tidal wetlands and estuarine ecosystems along the U.S. Gulf Coast since 2009.
1428 Laguna Shores Road, Suite 4Corpus Christi, TX 78418

visibleText: Brackwater Marsh Conservancy Mission Wetlands Volunteer Grants Contact Get in touch Contact the Conservancy Whether you're proposing a partnership, applying for a grant, joining a volunteer crew, or asking a question about Gulf Coast wetlands, our team is glad to hear from you. Reach us by mail, by email, or through the form below. Headquarters Brackwater Marsh Conservancy Coastal Field Office Street 1428 Laguna Shores Road, Suite 4 City Corpus Christi, TX 78418 Phone (361) 555-0142 General hello@brackwatermarsh.org Hours Mon–Fri, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CT Field Lab Open by appointment, Sat 10–2 Field office location 27.6648° N · 97.2698° W Visit & Mail Our office sits along the western shore of the Laguna Madre, fifteen minutes south of downtown Corpus Christi. Visitors are welcome during business hours; please call ahead if you plan to tour the native plant nursery or restoration lab. Quick Facts Free parking on-site, with two ADA-accessible spaces. Donations of native seed stock accepted year-round. Media inquiries: press@brackwatermarsh.org Tax ID (EIN): 27-3318094 — 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Mailing address differs from physical. Please send checks, applications, and physical correspondence to: P.O. Box 6612, Corpus Christi, TX 78466. All mail is reviewed within five business days. Staff Directory Six full-time staff coordinate restoration, education, grants, and field operations across the Texas and Louisiana Gulf Coast. Name Title Email Dr. Renata Solís Executive Director & Founder renata@brackwatermarsh.org Marcus Theriot Director of Restoration marcus@brackwatermarsh.org Aisha Bellamy Education & Outreach Manager aisha@brackwatermarsh.org Hollis Nguyen Grants Program Officer hollis@brackwatermarsh.org Jeremiah Pace Field Operations Coordinator jeremiah@brackwatermarsh.org Priya Ramachandran Development & Communications priya@brackwatermarsh.org Frequently Asked Questions Is my donation tax-deductible? Yes. Brackwater Marsh Conservancy is a registered 501(c)(3). All donations are tax-deductible to the fullest extent permitted by U.S. law. A receipt is emailed within 48 hours. Can I designate my gift to a specific project? Absolutely. During checkout, choose from restoration, education, or the Coastal Resilience Fellowship. Restricted gifts of $1,000+ may be tracked with quarterly impact updates. How do I sign up to volunteer? Visit our Volunteer page, review the eight open program roles, and submit an interest form. A coordinator follows up within five business days to schedule onboarding and orientation. Do volunteers need prior experience? Most roles require no prior experience — just curiosity and reliability. Specialized roles like Bird Survey Technician and Water Quality Sampler include free training sessions each spring. When are grant applications open? The Seed Fund accepts rolling applications. The Education Initiative opens each January, and the Fellowship cycle opens each March. See the Grants page for specific deadlines and required materials. Who is eligible to apply for grants? Eligibility varies by program. Most grants fund Gulf Coast nonprofits, educators, tribes, and emerging scientists working within Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, or the Florida Panhandle. Can my school or class visit the field office? Yes — we host K-12 group tours on Tuesdays and Thursdays during the school year. Email Aisha Bellamy at least three weeks in advance to coordinate scheduling and curriculum alignment. How do I report a wetland concern or fish kill? For active emergencies, contact Texas Parks & Wildlife first. For ongoing observations or restoration questions, email field@brackwatermarsh.org with location, date, and any photos. Send Us a Message Use the form below for general inquiries. For grant submissions or volunteer applications, please use the dedicated forms on those program pages. Full Name Email Address Subject Message (120 characters max) Send Message Response Times What to expect General 2–3 days Volunteer 5 days Grants 7–10 days Press 24 hours Prefer to talk? Call (361) 555-0142 weekdays between 9 AM and 5 PM Central. Leave a voicemail and we'll return your call within one business day.
